titleStmt.title: SII 38.350: original edition by S. Swaminathan
No. 350. (A.R. No. 350 of 1912.) VĪRALŪR, POLUR TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT. On a slab erected near the Gaṇēsa temple.

p.hi: King: Dēvarāyamahārāya II. Year: Kīlaka ie., Śaka 1351.

summary.p: It records that two individuals, Vidiyār Nāyakkar and Viṭṭappa Nāyakkar visited Viśaiyāndaram (Vijayanagaram) and obtained from (the king) four hamlets, Terindamutti, Śiṟupākkam, Taṇḍaṉapaṭṭi and Nilaiyiṭṭāṉ-ēndal, situated on the north bank of Paṅgaḷa-nāḍu, as sarvamānya-iṟaiyili to the god, Aruḷāḷanāthar and goddess Uṇṇāmalai-nāchchiyār and also obtained a charity-deed, during the regime of Deṇṇāyaka.

Further it is also stated that as these villages had dry lands and did not have wet lands to cultivate, they (temple authorities) were permitted to dig wells and excavate tanks to create wet lands and settle people which enable them to cultivate various crops.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
Aruḷāḷanātar Āṉa
nāyaṉārkku ciṟumuṟi . paṅ
kaḷanāṭu vaṭakarai te
rintamuttiyum ciṟupā
kkamum taṇṭaṉ
paṭṭi nilaiyiṭṭāṉ En
talum Inta E(eḷa)ntal nālum
vicaiyāntarattukkup po
y tevarāyar mabhRead mahāĀirāyar
pakkalile vitiyār nā
yakkarum tāmum viṭṭa
pparum Uṭaiyavarkku Inta Ūr
kaḷ tārāpūrvam Āka svāmā
nnya Iṟaiili Āka teyva maṇ
ṭalam Āka varaṅkamallappa te
ṇṇāyakka kalile kilakavaru
ṣam Āṭimātam mutalukku Ā
cantiṟāṟkastātiāka Inta Ūr
kaḷ naṭakka taṟmasāsaṉamu vā
ṅki varukaiyil Inta Ūr
kaḷil tammuṭaiya viḻukkā
ṭu Āṉa terintu kuttiyum ciṟu
pottakamum puṉcainilam
Oḻiṉtu nañcai nilam(la) I
rukkaiyil Ivvaruṣam Āṭimā
tam mutalukku Inta Ental I
raṇṭukkum tarmaeriyum ve
ṭṭi kiṇaṟum veṭṭi nañcai
nilamum kaṇṭu kuṭiyumeṟṟi nañ
caipuñcai vāṉpayir puṉpayir
māvaṭai maravaṭai maṟṟum
veṇṭum payirkaḷum payiṟ te
ytuRead ceytukoḷḷa kaṭavarākavum Inta
Ēntal nālukkum Āka niyamit
ta nācciyāruṇṇāmulai nā
cciyār Āṭiaruḷa nāḷ OṉṟukkuIncomplete.
Digital edition of SII 38.350 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
